arts.ttl: Ipswich keep safety hopes alive with victory at Bournemouth
arts.sum: Tenth-placed Bournemouth have now lost four of their last five league games, in a further blow to their European hopes, while 18th-placed Ipswich are nine points from safety. Evanilson initiated hopes of a comeback when he pounced on a rebound in the 67th minute, but Ipswich held firm to claim just their fourth league win of the season.

arts.ttl: We can’t control it – McKenna calm about escape bid despite overdue Ipswich win
arts.sum: Goals from Nathan Broadhead and top-scorer Liam Delap did the damage at Bournemouth.

arts.ttl: 'Everything is costing us a lot' - Iraola after Bournemouth defeat
arts.sum: Bournemouth manager Andoni Iraola says "everything is costing a lot" after the Cherries' poor form continued with a 2-1 Premier League defeat by Ipswich at the Vitality Stadium.

arts.ttl: McKenna 'really proud' after Ipswich beat Bournemouth
arts.sum: Ipswich manager Kieran McKenna is "really proud" of his side after a 2-1 Premier League win over Bournemouth at the Vitality Stadium.

arts.ttl: Delap nets winner for Ipswich at Bournemouth but survival hopes still faint
arts.athr: John Brewin at the Vitality Stadium
arts.sum: If Ipswich could have been forgiven for having next season’s Championship already in mind, Bournemouth are plunging their own depths of disappointment. Winless since mid-February, Cherry-flavoured European dreams fade fast, defeat to the strugglers following exit from the FA Cup. Still nine points shy of safety, Kieran McKenna’s team’s chances remain remote. The only certainty appears that Liam Delap, scorer of a rampaging second goal, will be playing Premier League football next season but, with eight games to play, after a first Premier League win in 2025, their small chance is far better than no chance at all. Ipswich, without a game since 15 March, have seen survival hopes crumble while they could only watch in impotent pessimism. The revivals of Everton, West Ham and most obviously Wolves have trampled on any seeds of a comeback; Wolves’ defeat of the Hammers on Tuesday was another dagger to hearts. Autumnal expectations have been crushed by a scolding winter, and some harsh lessons learned. Back in December, Ipswich took the game to Bournemouth only to be denied at the last. Here they set up with far greater pragmatism. Six changes from a 4-2 home defeat to Nottingham Forest suggested McKenna had done plenty of thinking. Continue reading...

arts.ttl: Bournemouth 1-2 Ipswich Town: Highlights, man of the match, stats as Liam Delap gives Kieran McKenna Premier League hope
arts.sum: Liam Delap starred as Kieran McKenna's relegation-threatened Ipswich Town earned a rare Premier League win against Andoni Iraola's Bournemouth.

arts.ttl: Nathan Broadhead on target as Ipswich beat Bournemouth for survival lifeline
arts.sum: The Cherries did get a goal back 23 minutes from time.

arts.ttl: ‘Revolution and rejuvenation’ – Manager may be done with Bournemouth player, exit seems likely
arts.sum: Bournemouth loanee Philip Billing isn’t earning too many fans in his loan spell at Napoli. He failed to impress against AC Milan at the weekend, giving away a penalty. While that spot-kick wasn’t converted by Santiago Gimenez, the Italian press didn’t spare the Dane and his showing. arts.ttl: No Kluivert for Bournemouth vs Ipswich LIVE!
arts.sum: Two draws and eight defeats into the year, they are now 12 points adrift as they visit Bournemouth tonight in their game in hand. If Ipswich want to have any hope in this survival fight, they need to start winning football matches now.

arts.ttl: Chelsea and Liverpool are 'battling it out to sign £50m-rated Bournemouth star' - but 'face fierce competition from Spanish giants Real Madrid'
arts.sum: Chelsea and Liverpool are both in the running to sign a Bournemouth star who has caught attention with his impressive performances for Andoni Iraola's side throughout the current campaign.
